title: sedum telephium var. rhodanense
intro: Sedum telephium var. rhodanense, also known as Rhodian stonecrop, is a perennial succulent plant that belongs to the Crassulaceae family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, skin problems, and inflammation, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Rhodian stonecrop include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, it is often used as a tincture, tea, or topical cream, and is also used in traditional folk medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
